title: 沉睡谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\140-沉睡谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Miss Marple discusses her interest in a revived murder case with Dr. Hedlock. Dr. Hedlock warns her against intervening, advising her to "let the sleeping murder sleep" because killers who have successfully covered their tracks may react dangerously if an investigation resumes. Miss Marple explains that her motive is not mere curiosity, but a desire to protect two young, inexperienced children involved in the matter. Dr. Hedlock expresses concern that her involvement in Dillesham might be a "death sentence."

Upon arriving in Dillesham, Miss Marple visits her friends, Colonel and Mrs. Bantry. While the Colonel is occupied with a gun and a Spanish hound, Mrs. Bantry discusses the Sanders, a former cook and her husband who used an inheritance to open an inn at Dillmouth Seafront, number 14. Mrs. Bantry encourages Miss Marple to stay at the inn for the sea air and food.

In a separate scene, Giles and Gwendolyn discuss a potential crime scene at a mountain villa. Gwendolyn recalls seeing a figure with non-human gray claws, but Giles remains skeptical, suggesting she is misremembering. Gwendolyn considers that the event might have been a childhood nightmare, noting that no one in Dillmouth has any record of a murder or disappearance occurring in that house. Giles reluctantly agrees it might be a dream, but he maintains his disbelief, arguing that one cannot dream specific lines from "The Duchess of Malfi."
